channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dead fox, 19th April
item.description:
Dead fox on street near Jey food and Wine (Anerley Road)
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Anerley Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped mattresses etc, 19th April
item.description:
Household goods, furniture etc. Fly tipped

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Old Farm Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large sections of the road between Cobbs Corner and Wells Park Road have fallen apart, 19th April
item.description:
This whole section of road is in a terrible state of disrepair with large areas where there is effectively no road surface. Deep ruts and potholes all over with only poor patch work visible. This is extremely dangerous for cyclists and likely to damage cars and other vehicles. It is a very busy road with lots of buses which has continue to degrade the surface. This whole stretch has needed proper resurfacing for years and has now effectively fallen apart in many places. Some serious remediation work is needed urgently please.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 275, Kirkdale, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Photo evidence of fly tipper identity, 19th April
item.description:
I have photos of personal items, addresses and NHS numbers on correspondence in the bin bags which were reported on 13 March at junction of Lavender Grove and Malvern Road. How do I share them with Hackney Council?
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Malvern Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bench is broken and needs repairing, 19th April
item.description:
The bench between the Perry Library and 251 Southwark Bridge Road has been damaged and needs repairing This has been damaged for a few days Looks like is will get worse/cause more issues if not repaired

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 241, Southwark Bridge Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London

item.title: 2 Ingham Road, 19th April
item.description:
This manhole outside 2 Ingham Road has had a HUGE hole along one side for too long. The manhole rocks and I am concerned someone or an animal will have an accident on or near it. The hole is very big!!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ingham Road, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip issue, 19th April
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Wadhurst Close, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Haverstock Hill, 19th April
item.description:
While I appreciate the efforts made to improve cycling safety in the area, the current measures are proving ineffective in practice. Drivers are routinely blocking and driving through the cycle lane, rendering it unsafe and, at times, unusable for cyclists. This is not an occasional issue, it is happening daily. The existing separation, consisting only of a kerb or raised path, is being widely ignored. I have personally witnessed vehicles, including Veolia lorries, repeatedly driving along the cycle lane on the wrong side of the road near the shops by Steeles Road. This situation undermines the purpose of the cycling infrastructure and creates a serious safety risk. Without more robust physical measures, such as bollards or other protective barriers, this behaviour will continue. I urge the council to take immediate action to implement effective safeguards that physically prevent vehicles from entering the cycle lane. The current design is not sufficient, and the risks to cyclists are significant and ongoing.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 10-16, Haverstock Hill, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The crossway is crumbling, 19th April
item.description:
This has been here over 2 years, previously reported and has got significantly worse. Fills with water when it rains, now causing damage to car wheels everytime we drive over to access our driveway, yet Foxgrove Road was completely replaced and these issues weren't present as i walk that road daily

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Warren Avenue, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More flytipped here, 19th April
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, Wagtail Way, St Paul's Cray, Greater London

item.title: Corner of Regents Park Rd & Fitzroy Rd, 19th April
item.description:
The kerb stones on the corner here have been driven over and are now a trip hazard and loose. It’s right by the crossing for Oldfield Estate so many older folks walking in this area. Please can you fix it ASAP.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 52, Fitzroy Road, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
